title: Why Spaced Repetition
description: Understand why spaced repetition dramatically improves learning, retention, and long-term mastery.

lessons.title: The Forgetting Curve
lessons.summary: Without review, most learning fades quickly.
content: Human memory decays rapidly unless information is revisited.
Most learners forget the majority of new information within days.
Spaced repetition interrupts this decay by reviewing material at increasing intervals.

practice.question: What typically happens to new learning without review?
practice.explanation: Without review, most new learning fades quickly.

practice.question: What does spaced repetition interrupt?
practice.explanation: Spaced repetition interrupts the normal decay of memory by revisiting material at increasing intervals.

lessons.title: Active Recall
content: Learning is strengthened when the brain retrieves information rather than rereading it.
This retrieval process strengthens neural pathways.

practice.question: Which is more effective for strengthening memory?
practice.explanation: Active recall works because retrieving information strengthens memory more than passive review.

practice.question: Why are flashcards useful?
options: They remove the need for review
practice.explanation: Flashcards are effective because they force retrieval, which strengthens memory.

lessons.title: The Spacing Effect
content: Reviewing information over time is more effective than cramming.
Each successful recall strengthens long-term memory.
Spaced repetition schedules reviews to maximize retention.

practice.question: What is generally more effective than cramming?
options: Studying once for a long time
practice.explanation: The spacing effect shows that reviewing over time is more effective than cramming.

practice.question: What does each successful recall strengthen?
practice.explanation: Each successful recall strengthens long-term memory.
